original: Leo II begins his reign as Catholic Pope
Yoritomo Minamoto leads uprising against Kiyomori Taira who installed his grandson, Emperor Antoku, to the throne
Peace of Bergerac: Political rights for Huguenots
Antwerp surrenders after an 8-month siege by the Duke of Parma
Battle of Preston, Lancashire: Henry Ireton defeats the Scots; ends August 19, 1648
France, Russia, and Prussia sign an agreement
Treaty of Åbo signed in Turku by Sweden and Russian Empire, ending the Russo-Swedish War (1741-1743)
Jews are granted permission in Budapest, Hungary, to pray in groups
Slave revolt at Knip Plantation, Curaçao
British defeat Batavian Navy in Saldanha Bay, South Africa
The Church Missionary Society establishes New Zealand's second mission station at Kerikeri
Solymon Merrick of Springfield, Massachusetts, patents the first wrench
British parliament accepts registration of births, marriages, and deaths
Charles Darwinleaves South America for the last time on HMS Beagle
Commodore Robert F. Stockton of the US Navy annexes California
The first bank in Hawaii opens
First airmail in a balloon takes off from Lafayette, Indiana
Confederate troops under Edmund Kirby Smith enter Kentucky
Federal batteries and ships attack Fort Sumter in South Carolina
First ascent of Mount Rainier, Washington, by Hazard Stevens and P. B. Van Trump
American astronomer Asaph Hall discovers Mars's moon Phobos
Ferdinand de Lesseps forms the French Panama Canal Company
The first public performance of the Dominican Republic's national anthem, Himno Nacional
Electric self-starter for automobiles patented
People's Bath, the first public bathhouse with showers, opens in New York City
Russian and French generals Obruchev and Boisdeffre sign the Dual Alliance
Phillies get 36 hits as Sam Thompson hits for the cycle, beating Louisville 29-4
W. B. Purvis patents electric railway switch (also known as points)
The Royal Titles Act adds the words 'and the British Dominions beyond the Seas' to the monarch's style
Boston's Jesse Tannehill no-hits the Chicago White Sox, 6-0
George H. Reid succeeds John Watson as Prime Minister of Australia
Dutch government of Theo de Meester is formed
Bishop forbids Christian membership of Dutch Textile Union
Projection in Paris of the first animated cartoon, "Fantasmagorie," created by Émile Cohl
French troops under General de Castelnau occupy Chateau Salins
A mob lynches Jewish businessman Leo Frank in Cobb County, Georgia, after his death sentence for the murder of a 13-year-old girl is commuted to life
German troops overrun Kaunas, Lithuania
Hurricane makes landfall near Galveston, Texas, killing 275 and causing $50 million of damage
Bulgarian offensive in Macedonia
Italy declares war on Turkey
Bolshevik revolutionary leader Moisei Uritsky is assassinated
British troops attack Baku, Azerbaijan
Samuel Riddle buys Man o'War for $5,000
Turkish troops overthrow Caucasus
New York Yankees cancel game with Cleveland Indians in memory of Ray Chapman, who dies after being hit by a pitch the previous day
French-German trade agreement signed
Greek-Serbian, Croatian and Slavs peace treaty signed
Soviet Union tests GIRD-R1 rocket ("Object 09")
An unemployed worker, Niels B. Ruud in Madison, Wisconsin, receives the first unemployment benefit check paid under a state law for $15
First aircraft owned by US Forest Service enters service in Oakland
Henry Armstrong wins his third consecutive boxing championship
German raider attacks Dutch SS Kota Nopan
Task Force 17 leaves Pearl Harbor under Admiral George Murray on USS Hornet
Transport #20 departs with French Jews to Nazi Germany
US 8th Air Force bombers stage their first independent raid on Europe, attacking Rouen, France
498 British bombers attack Peenemünde (development base for the V-weapons)
US 8th Air Force loses 60 B-17s during an attack on Schweinfurt and Regensburg
4th Canadian Armoured Division occupies Trun, Normandy
Soviet troops arrive at the Austria-Prussia border
US 12th Army Corps occupies Orléans
US 320th regiment infantry occupies Châteaudun
Yankees' Johnny Lindell ties the record with four consecutive doubles in a game
Korea is divided into North and South Korea along the 38th parallel
The Radcliffe Line is revealed, setting the border between Union of India and Dominion of Pakistan
Alger Hiss denies ever being a Communist agent
Phillies commit eight errors in a baseball game for the second time
Tom Henrich hits his record-tying fourth grand slam of the season
Pee Wee Reese (Dodgers) and Sam Calderone (Giants) hit inside-the-park home runs
18th NFL Chicago All-Star Game: Cleveland 33, All-Stars 0 (92,180 attendees)
Hurricane winds drive six ships ashore in Kingston, Jamaica
The word "fallout," referring to radioactive particles carried into the atmosphere after a nuclear explosion, is first used in The New York Times
First meeting of Narcotics Anonymous in Southern California
Hurricane Diane follows Hurricane Connie and floods the Connecticut River, killing 190 people and causing $1.8 billion in damage
20,000 women march to Pretoria's Union Buildings to present a petition to the Prime Minister against the carrying of passes by women
Federal Constitutional Court bans Communist Party of Germany (KPD) in West Germany
World's first Moon probe, USA's Thor-Able, explodes at T+77 seconds
7.1-magnitude earthquake strikes Yellowstone National Park
USSR and Iraq sign a contract to build an Iraqi nuclear reactor
Gabon gains independence from France (National Day)
Indonesia drops diplomatic relations with the Netherlands
Kennedy administration establishes Alliance for Progress
East German border guards shoot and kill Peter Fechter, 18, as he attempts to cross the Berlin Wall into the western sector
Jim Hickman becomes the first New York Met to hit for the cycle
Orioles' Dick Hall retires his 28th consecutive player in relief
Boycott scores his first Test cricket century: 113 against Australia at The Oval
NASA satellite Pioneer 7 launched into solar orbit
Dick Beyer (Dr. X) defeats Verne Gagne to become NWA champion
Hurricane Camille strikes the US coastline and kills 259 people, mainly in Alabama, Mississippi, and Louisiana
NY Jets beat NY Giants 37-14 in their first meeting (preseason)
Soviet spacecraft Venera 7 is launched towards Venus
Mets first basemanWillie Mayshits his 660th and last career home run off Don Gullett of the Cincinnati Reds in a 2-1 loss at Shea Stadium in New York
An earthquake and tsunami in the Philippines kill up to 8,000
West Indies win big at The Oval with Michael Holding taking 14-149 in the match
Russian nuclear-powered icebreaker "Arktika" is the first surface ship to reach the North Pole
Thomas Wessinghage, Harald Hudak, Michael Lederer, and Karl Fleschen set the world record in the 4 x 1500 m relay with a time of 14:38.8
USSR performs a nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
First successful crossing of the Atlantic by balloon is completed by Americans Ben Abruzzo, Maxie Anderson, and Larry Newman aboard Double Eagle II
Two Russian passenger planes collide over Ukraine, killing 173
Azaria Chamberlain disappears, likely taken by a dingo, leading to one of the most publicized trials in Australian history
George Brettgoes 4-for-4, raising his batting average to .401
First compact discs (CDs) released to the public in Germany
LA Dodgers beat the Chicago Cubs 6-5 in 21 innings; game ends the following day
South Bend, Indiana jury acquits self-avowed racist Joseph Paul Franklin
Hurricane Alicia kills 17 people in Texas
1,400 meatpackers walk off the job at a George A. Hormel & Company plant
Sara Trollinger forms House of Hope in Orlando
Bronze pig statue, named "Rachel" after a real 750-pound pig who won the 1985 Island County Fair, is unveiled at Pike Place Market, Seattle
Red Sox trade shortstop Rey Quinones and pitcher Mike Trujillo to Mariners for shortstop Spike Owen and outfielder Dave Henderson
Rioting occurs at a DMC concert at the Long Beach Convention Center in California; 40 people are injured
Dow Jones Industrial Average closes above 2,700 for the first time (2,700.57)
Butch Reynolds runs a world record in the 400 m (43.29)
First case of Rocky Mountain Spotted Fever in NYC (9-year-old Bronx boy)
LIRR says Penn Station will get air conditioning in 1991
Pakistani President Muhammad Zia-ul-Haq and US Ambassador Arnold Raphel are killed in a plane crash
US performs a nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
US Republicans nominate George H. W. Bush for President
Carlton Fisk hits White Sox record 187th home run
ArenaBowl V takes place at Joe Louis Arena in Detroit as the Tampa Bay Storm defeats the Detroit Drive 48-42, with Stevie Thomas named MVP
Royals' Warren Cromartie's first major league home run since 1983
Hurricane Andrew forms southeast of Barbados in the Caribbean
Kevin Gross of the Dodgers tosses the only 9-inning no-hitter of the season, a 2-0 victory
King Letsie II of Lesotho dismisses premier Ntsu Mokhehle
NY Central Park Reservoir officially named after Jackie Kennedy Onassis
China performs a nuclear test at Lop Nur, China
Soyuz TM-24 is launched into orbit
7.4-magnitude earthquake strikes İzmit, Turkey, killing more than 17,000 and injuring 44,000
Chinese researchers find an MD5 collision
The National Assembly of Serbia unanimously adopts new state symbols for Serbia: "Bože Pravde" becomes the new anthem, and the coat of arms is adopted for the whole country
Yana Klochkova of Ukraine wins the 200 m individual medley in 2:11.14 at the Athens Olympics, wrapping up the 200/400 m medley double
The first forced evacuation of settlers begins as part of Israel's unilateral disengagement plan

Australian women's 4 x 100 m medley relay team of Emily Seebohm, Leisel Jones, Jessicah Schipper, and Libby Trickett upsets the United States to win the gold medal in a world record time of 3:52.69 at the Beijing Olympics

China wins its 33rd gold medal of the Beijing Olympics by defeating Singapore in the table tennis women's team event, achieving its most successful Olympiad ever by winning all 10 singles and all 5 doubles matches

Four years after winning Olympic silver in the rowing quad sculls in Athens, Briton Rebecca Romero becomes only the second woman to earn medals in two different sports at the Summer Games when she wins the pursuit track cycling gold in Beijing
German swimmer Britta Steffen beats American Dara Torres by 0.01 in the 50m to wrap up the 50/100 m double at the Beijing Olympics
Gulnara Samitova-Galkina of Russia wins the 3,000 m steeplechase gold medal at the Beijing Olympics with a world record time of 8:58.81
Jamaica dominates the 100 m at the Beijing Olympics with Shelly-Ann Fraser taking the gold medal in 10.78, while her teammates Sherone Simpson and Kerron Stewart dead-heat for silver in 10.98
Gay pride events are banned for a century in Moscow
Three members of Russian punk band Pussy Riot are jailed for two years
15 civilians are killed by a Syrian warplane attack in Aleppo
18 people are killed in a conflict between Boko Haram and the Nigerian military
2012 Olympic 1500 m champion from Turkey, Asli Cakir Alptekin, is stripped of her gold medal by the IAAF for doping
Bomb blast in Bangkok at Erawan Shrine kills at least 19 and injures over 100
Discovery of a 7,000-year-old mass grave in Schöneck-Kilianstädten, Central Germany, is published in the PNAS Journal; 26 bodies bear evidence of violent conflict
World's first flower could be the underwater plant Montsechia vidalii, claim US botanists (Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences)

American sweep in the 100 m women's hurdles at the Rio de Janeiro Olympics with Brianna Rollins taking gold ahead of Nia Ali and Kristi Castlin; first time the US has swept medals in an Olympic women's track and field event
Floodwaters recede in Louisiana, leaving 13 dead and damaging 4,000 homes
German pair Laura Ludwig and Kira Walkenhorst upset Brazilian hometown favorites Ágatha Bednarczuk and Bárbara Seixas 21–18, 21–14 to win the Olympic women's beach volleyball gold in Rio de Janeiro
Jamaican sprinter Elaine Thompson wins the 200 m gold medal in 21.78, wrapping up the 100/200 m Rio de Janeiro Olympics double
Kenyan runner Conseslus Kipruto records an Olympic record 8:03.28 to win the men's 3,000 m steeplechase gold medal at the Rio de Janeiro Games
Anti-immigrant One Nation party leader Pauline Hanson is widely criticized for wearing a burqa into the Australian Parliament
Collision of two neutron stars is witnessed for the first time, first detected by the US-based Laser Interferometer Gravitational-Wave Observatory (LIGO)
On This Day helps launch Borneo Dictionary, an online nonprofit dictionary of indigenous Borneo languages with translations in English and Bahasa Malaysia
Terror attack on Las Ramblas in Barcelona, Spain, as a van rams into crowds, killing 16 and injuring 120
Three Hong Kong activists, Jason Wong, Nathan Law, and Alex Chow, are jailed for unlawful assembly
Huge wildfires begin on Gran Canaria in the Canary Islands, prompting the evacuation of 9,000 people
Landmark power-sharing deal signed by military and civilian leaders is celebrated in Khartoum, Sudan, with a transitional government to take over on September 1
Suicide bombing at a wedding in Kabul, Afghanistan, kills 63 and injures over 200; the Islamic State claims responsibility
University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill becomes the first US college to send students home and convert to online classes after 135 COVID-19 cases are detected
One in five US hospital ICUs is at or over 95% capacity due to the Delta surge of COVID-19, with 1,800 children hospitalized
China issues its highest red alert heat warning for at least 138 cities and counties amid the country's longest heatwave since records began, lasting 64 days
Possible second smaller impact crater that wiped out the dinosaurs, the "Nadir Crater," 8.5 km across, is identified off the coast of Guinea, West Africa
Suicide explosion in Afghan mosque in Kabul kills 21 worshippers and injures 33
UK inflation rate rises to a new 40-year high of 10.1%, with food costs as the largest contributor
First WHO Traditional Medicine Global Summit begins in Gandhinagar, Gujarat, India
US 30-year fixed mortgage rate rises above 7% for the first time in 21 years
An estimated 2.5 million people participate in protests across Israel, with 300,000 attending the rally in Tel Aviv, calling for a ceasefire and hostage deal in Gaza, Palestine
Bodhana Sivanandan (10) defeats grandmaster Pete Wells (60) in final round of 2025 British Chess Championships in Liverpool, England, becoming youngest female player to beat a grandmaster
French city of  Saint-Dizier seized by Imperial army of Holy Roman EmperorCharles Vduring Italian War (1842-46)
KingCharles IXof France declared an adult at 13
Francis, Duke of Anjou, visits EnglishQueen Elizabeth Iin an attempt to court her
Robert Fulton's steamboat Clermont begins its first trip up the Hudson River
French EmperorNapoleonasks his brother, the King of Holland Louis Bonaparte for a Dutch Brigade to fight on the French side against Spain in the Peninsular War
Charles Darwinreaches the top of Campana in Chile during his voyage on the Beagle
Richard Wagner's opera "Götterdämmerung" premieres in Bayreuth
Joseph Pulitzerdonates $1 million to Columbia University and begins the Pulitzer Prizes in the United States
Bank of Italy (nowBank of America) opens a new headquarters, the Bank of Italy building, at Clay and Montgomery, San Francisco
MLB playerLou Gehrigplays his record 1,308th consecutive game
Adolf Hitlerorders a total blockade of Great Britain
Franklin D. Rooseveltand Canadian Prime Minister William M. King agree to mutual defense abroad and establish the Permanent Joint Board of Defense in the Ogdensburg Agreement
US GeneralGeorge S. Pattonenters Messina, completing the Allied conquest of Sicily
German field marshal Walter Model replacesGünther von Klugein Normandy
Sukarnoand Mohammad Hatta declare Indonesia (formerly the Dutch East Indies) independent from the Netherlands
Arthur Honegger's 3rd Symphony "Liturgique" premieres in Zürich, Switzerland with Charles Munch conducting the Suisse Romande Orchestra
George Orwellpublishes "Animal Farm" in the United Kingdom

Baseball playerRichie Ashburnfouls and hits fan Alice Roth twice in the same at-bat while playing for the Philadelphia Phillies; the first hit breaks her nose, and the second one hits her while she is on the stretcher

Columbia Records releases "Kind of Blue," a studio album by American jazz trumpeter and composerMiles Davis, leading a sextet featuring saxophonistJohn Coltraneand pianist Bill Evans; the work is widely considered a masterpiece and is one of the most influential and best-selling albums of Davis' long career
Francis Gary Powers' U-2 spy trial opens in Moscow
San Francisco Giants outfielderWillie Maystakes second place on the all-time home run list
MLB Philadelphia Phillies'Steve Carltonwins his 15th consecutive game
American golferLee Trevinohits his first hole-in-one
"Monty Python's Life of Brian" directed by Terry Jones, starring Graham Chapman,Michael Palin, andJohn Cleese, and funded byGeorge Harrison's HandMade Films, premieres in US theaters
Pete Rosereturns to the Cincinnati Reds as a player-manager and gets two hits in the game
Rajiv Gandhiannounces Punjab State elections in India
Bunt single gives Milwaukee Brewers'Paul Molitorthe longest MLB hit streak of the 1980s at 39 games
Muhammad Aliis elected to "Ring" magazine's Boxing Hall of Fame
Baltimore Orioles shortstopCal Ripkenplays in his 1,208th consecutive game and moves to 3rd place all-time behind NY Yankees' Lou Gehrig and Boston Red Sox/NY Yankees' Everett Scott
"Exorcist3," a film written and directed byWilliam Peter Blatty, premieres
Phyllis Polander suesMike Tysonfor sexual harassment
Dutch AC Milan manager and former playerMarco van Bastenannounces his retirement from professional football
Perez Hiltonmistakenly announces the death of Cuban leaderFidel Castro
Rafael Nadalof Spain beats Chile's Fernando González 6-3, 7-6, 6-3 to win the men's tennis singles at the Beijing Olympics, Spain's first-ever Olympic gold medal in tennis
The Tradition Senior Men's Golf, Crosswater Club:Fred Funkwins first of 3 Champions Tour majors by 3 strokes from Mike Goodes

The US team ofMichael Phelps,Aaron Peirsol, Brendan Hansen, and Jason Lezak wins the 4 x 100 m medley relay at the Beijing Olympics in a world record time of 3:29.34; Phelps's 8th gold medal of the Games breaks Mark Spitz's 1972 record of 7
Joel Embiidsigns a four-year, $196 million NBA extension with the 76ers

French Superintendent of FinancesNicolas Fouquetthrows one of the grandest and most opulent parties ever seen in France, appalling King Louis XIV and leading to Fouquet's arrest for embezzlement weeks later
Musical film "The Wizard of Oz," starringJudy Garland, Ray Bolger, Bert Lahr, Jack Haley, and Margaret Hamilton, opens at Loew's Capitol Theatre, NYC

Monica Lewinsky scandal: US PresidentBill Clintonadmits in taped testimony he had an "improper physical relationship" with the intern and on the same day admits before the nation he "misled people" about the relationship
Richard of Shrewsbury, Duke of York, son of King Edward IV, one of the princes in the tower who disappeared, born in Shrewsbury, England (d. 1483?)
